description: Create the report generation module (src/augur/report.py) that transforms analysis results into a structured Chinese Markdown deep analysis report
steps: Create src/augur/report.py with a generate_report(ticker, context, results, consensus) function
The function takes: ticker (str), context (MarketContext), results (Dict[str, AgentResponse]), consensus (AgentResponse) and returns a Markdown string

Report structure should follow the pattern in cases/analysis-reports/example-analysis-costco.md with these sections: 1) Title + metadata (date, method), 2) Executive Summary with scorecard/signal/score/position, 3) Agent Consensus Table showing all 18 agents' signal/score/confidence, 4) Detailed Findings organized by theme (value/growth/risk/moat/technical), 5) Risk Assessment matrix, 6) Position Recommendation with Kelly sizing

Group agent findings by investment theme: Value agents (buffett, graham, munger, duan_yongping, li_lu, dan_bin), Growth agents (cathie_wood, fisher, lynch, aschenbrenner, thiel), Risk/Macro agents (dalio, soros, marks, serenity), Technical agents (arps, dayu), China specialists (zhang_lei, duan_yongping, li_lu, dan_bin)
Use Chinese for all report content and section headers. Include market context data (PE, PB, ROE, etc) where available
Include the signal distribution counts (bullish/neutral/bearish) and generate a summary scoring table similar to the Costco example's scoring card

Add helper functions: _format_signal_chinese(signal) to convert 'bullish' to Chinese labels, _format_agent_table(results) for the agent breakdown table, _format_theme_section(theme_name, agent_ids, results) for themed analysis sections
Handle edge cases: empty results, all-error agents, missing context data
acceptance_criteria: generate_report returns a non-empty Markdown string with Chinese content
Report contains all section headers (executive summary, agent table, themed findings, risks, recommendation)
All 18 agents are represented in the agent consensus table
Works with minimal context data (just ticker name)
